mission.title: kube-burner: [RFE] Ability to template the kind field of a kubernetes object
mission.description: [RFE] Ability to template the kind field of a kubernetes object. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-burner deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kube-burner version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-burner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-burner
helm list -n kube-burner 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kube-burner installation.

steps.title: Review kube-burner configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-burner configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-burner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-burner
kubectl get configmap -n kube-burner -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-burner
```
While attempting to create a single job that can create a number of CRDs and CRs with minimal number of pre-created yaml manifests, I discovered it seems not possible to have template language in the `kind` field of an object.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFE] Ability to template the kind field of a kubernetes…
steps.description:
This specific issue has been addressed by https://github.com/kube-burner/kube-burner/pull/999, can you confirm if that solution works for you  ?

Thanks!
```yaml
---
global:
  gc: false

jobs:
- name: test-crd-cr
  jobType: create
  jobIterations: 5
  namespacedIterations: false
  cleanup: true
  podWait: false
  qps: 10
  burst: 10
  objects:
  - objectTemplate: crd.yml
    replicas: 1

  - objectTemplate: cr.yml
    replicas: 1
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-burner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-burner
kubectl get events -n kube-burner --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[RFE] Ability to template the kind field of a kubernetes…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This specific issue has been addressed by https://github.com/kube-burner/kube-burner/pull/999, can you confirm if that solution works for you  ?

Thanks!
codeSnippets:
---
global:
  gc: false

jobs:
- name: test-crd-cr
  jobType: create
  jobIterations: 5
  namespacedIterations: false
  cleanup: true
  podWait: false
  qps: 10
  burst: 10
  objects:
  - objectTemplate: crd.yml
    replicas: 1

  - objectTemplate: cr.yml
    replicas: 1

---
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: testcrds{{.Iteration}}.testcrd.example.com
spec:
  group: testcrd.example.com
  versions:
    - name: v1
      served: true
      storage: true
      schema:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            spec:
              type: object
              properties:
                workload:
                  type: string
                iterations:
                  type: integer
  scope: Namespaced
  names:
    plural: testcrds{{.Iteration}}
    singular: testcrd{{.Iteration}}
    kind: TestCRD{{.Iteration}}
    shortNames:
    - tcrd{{.Iteration}}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-burner installed or the issue environment reproducible.
